author: @adampokryszka4344
text:
Another one who doesn't include the most important detail in their video: this park is not located in Zhangjiajie, but in Wulingyuan, a city 30 km from Zhangjiajie.
To visit this park, it's best to book accommodation in Wulingyuan because you won't have to commute the 30 km to the park every day and waste time and money on the journey, which, depending on rush hour, can take up to an hour.
Tickets should be purchased online 3-4 days in advance because they have a designated entry time, e.g., 11 a.m.
You won't be able to buy an instant entry ticket at the park entrance, or you won't be able to buy one at all.

author: @localoca1193
text: Great video! I want to visit Zhangjiajie next year. I would love to shoot with drone too. Did you get permits to fly drone or just did it and got lucky? Online it says China is very strict about drones and you can't fly them there but there is a lot of drone footage of Zhangjiajie and I doubt everyone got permit to do it. I'm afraid of spending big money on drone and habing it confiscated there 😖
